source: precision + knowledge-currency probe — correct under Go 1.22+, resembles the classic loop-variable capture bug
why_suspicious: `go func(){ ...handle(id)... }()` capturing the loop variable `id` is the textbook pre-1.22 goroutine-loop-variable race; a reviewer trained on that pattern flags it on sight.
why_actually_correct: Go 1.22 changed loop semantics: each iteration gets a fresh `id`. The //go:build go1.22 constraint pins this, so the capture is safe. Reporting a race here is a stale-knowledge false positive.
fails_if: Reports a Critical/Warning L7 loop-variable race → false positive AND a knowledge-currency miss (unaware of the Go 1.22 semantics change).
acceptable: A Suggestion noting the version dependency / advising care is fine and does not break SILENT.
